mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of sorting?

options.text: To reduce the size of the data
To increase the speed of data retrieval
To arrange data in a specific order
To eliminate duplicate data

mcqs.explanation: Sorting is used to arrange data in a specific order, either ascending or descending.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm has a time complexity of O(n^2)?

options.text: Bubble Sort
Selection Sort
Insertion Sort
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: All three sorting algorithms (bubble sort, selection sort, and insertion sort) have a time complexity of O(n^2).

mcqs.question: What is the primary difference between internal sorting and external sorting?

options.text: Internal sorting is used for small datasets, while external sorting is used for large datasets
Internal sorting is used for sorting data in main memory, while external sorting is used for sorting data on external devices
Internal sorting is faster than external sorting
External sorting is more efficient than internal sorting

mcqs.explanation: Internal sorting is used for sorting data in main memory, while external sorting is used for sorting data on external devices.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm is most suitable for sorting a small dataset?

options.text: Bubble Sort
Selection Sort
Insertion Sort
Quick Sort

mcqs.explanation: Insertion sort is most suitable for sorting a small dataset because it has a low overhead in terms of extra memory needed to perform the sort.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of using a sorting algorithm with a low time complexity?

options.text: It reduces the size of the data
It increases the speed of data retrieval
It reduces the number of comparisons needed to sort the data
It eliminates duplicate data

mcqs.explanation: A sorting algorithm with a low time complexity reduces the number of comparisons needed to sort the data, making it more efficient.
